title: Texas Instruments Incorporated (TXN) Economic Prospect Score — Consensus Analysis
description: Consensus economic prospect analysis of Texas Instruments Incorporated (TXN), averaging independent scores from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 across competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment catalysts.
verdictDetail: Texas Instruments Incorporated receives a score of 60/100, reflecting its current competitive position and forward outlook. The company demonstrates solid fundamentals with some areas of concern that moderate the overall assessment.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Texas Instruments Incorporated shows moderate competitive momentum.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Texas Instruments Incorporated's revenue growth is in line with industry peers.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Market share has been stable in key segments.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: The company demonstrates strong pricing power in its markets.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Product innovation and launch cadence is above average.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Texas Instruments Incorporated's competitive moat is moderate.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Customer switching costs are moderate due to integration depth.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: IP portfolio and regulatory position provide strong competitive protection.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Scale advantages create meaningful barriers to entry.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment toward Texas Instruments Incorporated is neutral.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analyst estimates have been relatively stable.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: Media and analyst narrative is neutral.

keyRisks: Automotive EV slowdown reducing chip demand
Capacity overbuild risk at new Richardson fab
keyCatalysts: Automotive chip content per vehicle increasing 8% annually
New 300mm fab capacity enabling margin expansion
methodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored TXN at 60/100 and Opus at 61/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
